name: Solar System
subtitle: The Sun and its planets
blurb: Our solar system: the Sun and the eight planets that orbit it. Tap any planet to explore it up close and in AR.

name: The Sun
subtitle: Our nearest star
blurb: The Sun is the star at the centre of the solar system and keeps every planet in orbit.

funFacts.label: A giant star
funFacts.text: The Sun is a star — a giant ball of glowing gas so big that about 1.3 million Earths could fit inside it!

funFacts.label: Super hot
funFacts.text: The Sun's surface is around 5,500°C. Even from 150 million km away, it keeps our whole planet warm.

funFacts.label: Light takes 8 minutes
funFacts.text: Sunlight travels so far that it takes about 8 minutes to reach Earth — so you always see the Sun as it looked 8 minutes ago!

name: Mercury
subtitle: Closest to the Sun
blurb: The smallest planet and the closest to the Sun.

funFacts.label: Closest to the Sun
funFacts.text: Mercury is the planet nearest the Sun, racing all the way around it in just 88 days.

funFacts.label: Hot and cold
funFacts.text: Days on Mercury are scorching hot and nights are freezing cold, because it has almost no air to hold the heat.

funFacts.label: Smallest planet
funFacts.text: Mercury is the smallest planet — only a little bigger than our Moon.

name: Venus
subtitle: The hottest planet
blurb: A rocky planet about the same size as Earth, wrapped in thick clouds.

funFacts.label: The hottest planet
funFacts.text: Venus is the hottest planet of all, even hotter than Mercury, because thick clouds trap the heat like a blanket.

funFacts.label: Spins backwards
funFacts.text: Venus spins the opposite way to most planets, so there the Sun would rise in the west!

funFacts.label: Earth's twin
funFacts.text: Venus is almost the same size as Earth, which is why it is sometimes called Earth's twin.

name: Earth
subtitle: Our home planet
blurb: The only world we know of with oceans of water and living things.

funFacts.label: Our home
funFacts.text: Earth is the only planet we know of with life — and the only one with oceans of liquid water on its surface.

funFacts.label: Just right
funFacts.text: Earth sits at the perfect distance from the Sun: not too hot and not too cold, so water can stay liquid.

funFacts.label: One Moon
funFacts.text: Earth has one Moon, whose pull makes the ocean tides rise and fall every day.

name: Mars
subtitle: The Red Planet
blurb: A cold, dusty desert world that humans hope to visit one day.

funFacts.label: The Red Planet
funFacts.text: Mars looks red because its soil is full of rusty iron — like the rust on an old bike!

funFacts.label: Tallest volcano
funFacts.text: Mars has the tallest volcano in the solar system, Olympus Mons — more than twice as tall as Mount Everest.

funFacts.label: Two tiny moons
funFacts.text: Mars has two little potato-shaped moons called Phobos and Deimos.

name: Jupiter
subtitle: The biggest planet
blurb: A giant planet made mostly of gas, with a famous swirling storm.

funFacts.label: The biggest planet
funFacts.text: Jupiter is the largest planet — more than 1,300 Earths could fit inside it!

funFacts.label: A giant storm
funFacts.text: Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a storm bigger than the whole Earth that has been spinning for hundreds of years.

funFacts.label: Lots of moons
funFacts.text: Jupiter has dozens of moons. The four biggest were first spotted by Galileo over 400 years ago.

name: Saturn
subtitle: The ringed planet
blurb: A gas giant famous for its bright, beautiful rings.

funFacts.label: Beautiful rings
funFacts.text: Saturn's rings are made of billions of pieces of ice and rock, from tiny grains to huge boulders.

funFacts.label: It could float
funFacts.text: Saturn is so light for its size that, with a big enough bathtub, it would float in water!

funFacts.label: Windy world
funFacts.text: Winds on Saturn can blow many times faster than the strongest hurricanes on Earth.

name: Uranus
subtitle: The sideways planet
blurb: An icy giant with a blue-green colour that spins on its side.

funFacts.label: Rolls on its side
funFacts.text: Uranus is tipped right over, so it rolls around the Sun on its side like a ball.

funFacts.label: Blue-green colour
funFacts.text: A gas called methane in its air gives Uranus its pretty blue-green colour.

funFacts.label: Very chilly
funFacts.text: Uranus is one of the coldest planets, with temperatures dropping below -200°C.

name: Neptune
subtitle: The farthest planet
blurb: The most distant planet, a deep-blue, windy ice giant.

funFacts.label: The windiest planet
funFacts.text: Neptune has the fastest winds in the solar system — faster than 2,000 km/h!

funFacts.label: Farthest planet
funFacts.text: Neptune is the most distant planet, so far away that sunlight there is very dim.

funFacts.label: Deep blue
funFacts.text: Neptune's deep blue colour also comes from methane gas in its atmosphere.
